title: Worker reputation + pseudonymous credit ledger
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. This backlog task strengthens the never-trust-one-worker rule with a pseudonymous worker credit/reputation ledger that stores no PII and is opt-in, consistent with the project's privacy guardrails.
objective: Implement a pseudonymous worker credit/reputation ledger that strengthens never-trust-one-worker without storing PII.
acceptanceCriteria: Adds a pseudonymous worker credit/reputation ledger that strengthens the never-trust-one-worker rule.
Stores no PII; participation is opt-in; no secrets in logs or artifacts; MIT-licensed.
output: A PR adding the pseudonymous worker reputation and credit ledger.
